title: Every LLM invocation has auditable context artifact

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
**As a** Plexus developer debugging console chat issues  
**I want** an audit trail of the exact message list sent to the LLM for each invocation  
**So that** I can reproduce context-related failures and verify what the model actually saw

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: LLM context audit trail
  Every LLM invocation must have a persistent audit artifact
  capturing the exact messages sent to the model.

  Scenario: Single-call turn creates audit artifact
    Given I have a console chat session
    When I send a message that completes in one LLM call
    Then an audit artifact is uploaded to S3
    And the artifact contains the exact ordered message list sent to the model
    And the artifact includes model config (ID, temperature, etc)
    And the artifact includes token statistics
    And the artifact includes timestamps and latency
    And the ChatMessage metadata contains the S3 key for the artifact
    And the artifact has 14-day lifecycle policy

  Scenario: Multi-call turn with tools creates multiple artifacts
    Given I have a console chat session
    When I send a message that triggers tool use
    And the assistant makes 3 LLM calls (initial, tool follow-up, synthesis)
    Then 3 audit artifacts are uploaded to S3
    And each artifact captures a distinct call_index (0, 1, 2)
    And each artifact specifies call_type (initial, tool_followup, synthesis)
    And the ChatMessage metadata contains all 3 S3 keys
    And all artifacts are retrievable

  Scenario: Audit artifact is retrievable for debugging
    Given a console chat turn has completed
    And audit artifacts exist in S3
    When I fetch the ChatMessage metadata
    Then I can extract the contextArtifacts array
    And I can download each artifact from S3
    And each artifact parses as valid JSON
    And contains the complete message history sent to the model
```
